sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Nothing changed except the timer (c px=10 → c px=12). The G area stayed at px=2600, and all game objects are in the same positions. Clicking on the white area to the right of the objects didn't do anything.
Let me reconsider. The only click that caused a meaningful change was the first click on white at (15, 45). Let me think about what was special about that position.
Looking at the initial state:
- The upper white area was at cols 32-63, rows 1-23 (approximately)
- The click was at (15, 45), which is in the upper white area
After that click:
- The G area expanded left from col 31 to col 27 and right from col 51 to col 55
- The upper white area shifted left from col 32 to col 28
- The charcoal shape and yellow strip moved right by 4 columns
Wait, I think I need to reconsider the mechanics. Maybe the game isn't about pushing objects. Maybe clicking on white converts it to dark gray, and the objects move as a side effect of the boundary changing.
Actually, let me look at this differently. Maybe the game is about "filling" or "painting" the white areas with dark gray, and the goal is to completely fill all white areas. Or maybe the goal is to fill white areas until the charcoal shape touches something specific.
Let me try clicking on different white areas to see if they get converted. The white areas are:
1. Upper white: rows 1-23, cols 28-63 (approximately)
2. Lower white: rows 32-63, cols 56-63 (approximately)
Let me try clicking on the lower white area.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Only the first click on white at (15, 45) caused meaningful change - converted white to dark gray and shifted objects right. Subsequent clicks on white had no effect. Need to find the right clicking pattern.
Plan: Try clicking on the lower white area to see if it converts to dark gray.
